entries.title: Commentary on Proverbs on proverbs 18:13
entries.excerpt: He who answers before he hears, etc. He who wants to be a teacher before he learns does not avoid the fault of foolishness. He who judges the deeds of his neighbors before he fully understands the cause of both parties hastily acts improperly, and he shows himself worthy of confusion.

entries.title: The Desert Fathers, Sayings of the Early Christian Monks on proverbs 18:13
entries.excerpt: A brother said to Poemen, 'If I see something wrong do you want me to tell you about it?' He said to him, 'It is written, "If a man answers before he has heard, it is foolishness to him and discredit" (Sirach 11:8). If you are asked, speak; if not, say nothing.'
